 {"id":"bd-2y30","title":"E2E: degraded environment + permissions","description":"## Goal
Validate user-facing behavior when required tools are missing or permissions are insufficient.

## Scope
- Missing `gum`/`jq` detection and guidance messages.
- Permission denied reading `/proc` (simulate with sandbox/permissions), ensure actionable error.
- Read-only or missing config/data dirs (XDG overrides) with clear remediation.
- Negative paths: unsupported platform features (e.g., deep scan on unsupported OS) produce clear errors.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages are clear and actionable.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, error_kind, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: stderr snapshots, diagnostics summary, manifest.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing dependencies produce clear guidance and non-zero exit codes.
- [ ] Permission errors produce actionable remediation steps.
- [ ] Unsupported feature paths return clear errors and safe exits.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- E2E script using shared harness; simulate environment by PATH manipulation and read-only dirs.

 {"id":"bd-2yz0","title":"Unify threshold naming with --min-posterior alias","description":"## Task
Add `--min-posterior` as the canonical name for the posterior threshold flag, with `--threshold` as a backward-compatible alias.

## Background
The CLI specification uses `--min-posterior` while some implementations use `--threshold`. Both terms refer to the minimum posterior probability threshold for candidate selection.

For consistency with:
- Bayesian terminology (posterior probability)
- `agent apply --min-posterior` (if it exists)
- Academic/statistical convention

We should make `--min-posterior` the canonical name.

## Implementation

### Current (inconsistent)
```rust
/// Minimum posterior threshold
#[arg(long, default_value = "0.7")]
threshold: f64,
```

### Updated (canonical + alias)
```rust
/// Minimum posterior probability threshold for candidate selection
#[arg(long = "min-posterior", visible_alias = "threshold", default_value = "0.7")]
min_posterior: f64,
```

### Help Text Display
```
--min-posterior <VALUE>  Minimum posterior probability threshold [default: 0.7]
                         [aliases: threshold]
```

## Commands to Update
1. `agent plan` - Primary target (DONE if implemented)
2. `agent apply` - Verify consistency
3. Any other commands using threshold terminology

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--min-posterior` is the primary flag name in help
- [ ] `--threshold` continues to work (backward compatibility)
- [ ] Both flags set the same internal value
- [ ] Help text shows alias relationship
- [ ] No breaking changes for existing scripts using --threshold
- [ ] Variable name in code is `min_posterior` (not `threshold`)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_min_posterior_is_primary_name() {
    // Help shows --min-posterior as primary
}

#[test]
fn test_threshold_is_alias() {
    // --threshold parses and sets same value as --min-posterior
}

#[test]
fn test_default_value_is_0_7() {
    // Default threshold is 0.7
}

#[test]
fn test_both_flags_cannot_be_used_together() {
    // Using both --min-posterior and --threshold is either:
    // - An error, or
    // - Last one wins (clap default)
    // Document expected behavior
}
```

### Integration Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_plan_with_min_posterior() {
    // pt agent plan --min-posterior 0.9
    // Verify threshold is applied correctly
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_with_threshold_alias() {
    // pt agent plan --threshold 0.9
    // Same behavior as --min-posterior
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_output_shows_threshold_used() {
    // Output JSON includes the threshold value used
}
```

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_threshold_naming.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for threshold naming unification

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_threshold_naming_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: threshold naming ==="

# Test --min-posterior flag works
log "Testing --min-posterior flag..."
if pt-core agent plan --min-posterior 0.95 --format json > /tmp/output.json 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --min-posterior 0.95 accepted (exit 0)"
else
    EXIT_CODE=$?
    # Non-zero is OK if it is just "no candidates" - check output
    if grep -q "error" /tmp/output.json 2>/dev/null; then
        log "✗ --min-posterior flag not recognized"
        cat /tmp/output.json >> "$LOG"
        exit 1
    fi
    log "✓ --min-posterior flag accepted (exit $EXIT_CODE, likely no candidates)"
fi

# Test --threshold alias still works
log "Testing --threshold alias..."
if pt-core agent plan --threshold 0.85 --format json > /tmp/output.json 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --threshold alias works (exit 0)"
else
    EXIT_CODE=$?
    if grep -q "error" /tmp/output.json 2>/dev/null; then
        log "✗ --threshold alias failed"
        cat /tmp/output.json >> "$LOG"
        exit 1
    fi
    log "✓ --threshold alias accepted (exit $EXIT_CODE)"
fi

# Test default value is 0.7
log "Testing default value..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --format json 2>&1 || true)
# Check if threshold_used or equivalent field shows 0.7
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".threshold_used == 0.7 or .min_posterior == 0.7" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Default threshold is 0.7"
else
    log "⚠ Could not verify default (field may have different name or not in output)"
fi

# Test help shows --min-posterior as primary
log "Testing help output..."
HELP=$(pt-core agent plan --help 2>&1)
if echo "$HELP" | grep -q "min-posterior"; then
    log "✓ Help shows --min-posterior"
else
    log "✗ Help should show --min-posterior"
    exit 1
fi

# Test help shows threshold as alias
if echo "$HELP" | grep -qi "threshold"; then
    log "✓ Help mentions threshold (as alias)"
else
    log "⚠ Help might not show alias (acceptable if alias works)"
fi

# Test both flags produce same results (structure, not values that vary)
log "Testing equivalence..."
OUTPUT1=$(pt-core agent plan --min-posterior 0.8 --format json 2>&1 | jq -S "del(.session_id, .timestamp, .generated_at)" 2>/dev/null || echo "{}")
OUTPUT2=$(pt-core agent plan --threshold 0.8 --format json 2>&1 | jq -S "del(.session_id, .timestamp, .generated_at)" 2>/dev/null || echo "{}")

# Compare structure (fields present), not exact values
FIELDS1=$(echo "$OUTPUT1" | jq -r "keys | sort | .[]" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
FIELDS2=$(echo "$OUTPUT2" | jq -r "keys | sort | .[]" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

if [ "$FIELDS1" = "$FIELDS2" ]; then
    log "✓ Both flags produce same output structure"
else
    log "⚠ Output structure differs (may be due to timing/candidates)"
fi

log "=== E2E Test PASSED ==="
```

## Migration Notes

### For Scripts/Agents
- No action required - `--threshold` continues to work
- Consider migrating to `--min-posterior` for clarity
- Both flags will remain supported indefinitely

### For Documentation
- Primary examples should use `--min-posterior`
- Note alias for users familiar with `--threshold`","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBeaver","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:38:40.504081998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:15:28.645225750Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yz0","depends_on_id":"bd-v5ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:54Z","created_by":"import"}]}
